_comment: S1 SNR Ladder — Gage R&R + Bias/Linearity for SNR response (STUDY-SPEC §6). Redesigned 2026-06-06 per R&R-005: ladder restricted to reliable decode range (SNR >= -12 dB) to eliminate threshold-miss contamination of the two-way ANOVA. Sub-threshold SNRs (-24 to -15 dB) are now measured as decode rate in companion scenario S1b. Revised 2026-06-10 per R&R-006: each part receives a unique sub-dB fractional offset to break integer-quantization binning in the bias/linearity chart. Even-indexed parts receive ascending offsets {0.0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8}; odd-indexed parts receive descending offsets {0.9, 0.7, 0.5, 0.3, 0.1}. The interleaving yields near-zero Spearman rank-correlation between offset and SNR (r≈0.03), avoiding any false linearity signal. The ~3 dB step structure and -12 to +15 dB operating range are preserved.
name: SNR ladder
description: 10 parts × 2 appraisers × 3 trials. Drives: SNR R&R, bias, and linearity. R&R-005 (2026-06-06): parts restricted to {-12 ... +15} dB where both apps decode >= 95% of trials, eliminating threshold-miss contamination of the ANOVA. R&R-006 (2026-06-10): parts given unique sub-dB fractional offsets (even parts: ascending {0.0,0.2,0.4,0.6,0.8}; odd parts: descending {0.9,0.7,0.5,0.3,0.1}) to resolve integer-quantization binning in the bias/linearity chart; yields 10 uniformly-spaced expected bias levels across the D-002 region with negligible false linearity (r≈0.03).
